methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Linanza, ticker LNZA) operating in the same sector and adjacent stages, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $11M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage step-ups, implying a $28M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Watershed at $1.8B, Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M. The median across these four peers is $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $23M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $101M. The substantial width of this range—particularly the upper bound driven by the Watershed outlier—reflects material uncertainty around Furno's market opportunity and competitive positioning within a heterogeneous peer set. Key uncertainties include technology maturity, capital requirements to scale, and whether peer valuations reflect durable competitive advantages or sector-wide enthusiasm.
